content: Ericsson deliberate practice for learning apps requires: (1) Feedback after each attempt - immediate, specific, actionable; (2) Challenge at the edge of competence (not too easy, not too hard); (3) Progress tracking/goal monitoring that is public or visible; (4) Repetition with focused attention; (5) Working on weaknesses, not strengths. Key implication: auto-detecting mastery from real performance data (not self-report) aligns with deliberate practice because it provides objective external feedback. Self-assessment quizzes for conceptual nodes also align if they push learners to recall and apply knowledge.
